user_query:
I’ve been journaling sporadically and want to get back into a rhythm. Please do the following using the files I’ve provided and write all your outputs under output/:

Context and inputs
- Read the past entries under input/journal/. Assume today’s date is 2026-03-08.
- Do not modify anything in input/.

Deliverables
1) New daily entry (today):
   - Create output/journal/2026-03-08.md.
   - Use exactly one rotating reflective prompt from this list and place it on its own line starting with: "Prompt: <prompt>"
     • What was the highlight of your day?
     • What was challenging today?
     • What did you learn today?
     • Name one thing you're grateful for.
     • Describe today in three words.
     • What are you looking forward to?
     • What would you do differently today?
     • Who made a positive impact on your day?
     • What surprised you today?
     • What's on your mind right now?
   - Include a timestamped entry header line like: "## 20:30 | Mood: <Positive|Neutral|Negative>" (choose one mood label).
   - Write at least 80 words of reflection that genuinely responds to the chosen prompt.
   - Use English and a natural, reflective tone.

2) Weekly stats JSON:
   - Create output/weekly/2026-03-08_stats.json with keys: daysWritten, totalWords, avgWords (all integers).
   - Compute these across all available files from input/journal plus today’s new entry (i.e., all dates you have between 2026-03-01 and 2026-03-08 inclusive).
   - Use a simple word count rule: count words by splitting on whitespace across the full contents of each file; avgWords should be the rounded integer of totalWords/daysWritten.

3) Weekly reflection and pattern insights:
   - Create output/weekly/2026-03-01_to_2026-03-08.md titled "Weekly Reflection (2026-03-01 to 2026-03-08)".
   - Include sections with these headings: "Highlights", "Challenges", "Patterns & Trends", "Suggestions".
   - In Patterns & Trends, analyze mood and themes from the inputs by day of week (e.g., Monday, Tuesday, etc.) and note any recurring patterns you detect.
   - In Suggestions, give at least three concrete recommendations to improve next week’s well-being and consistency.

Important notes
- Keep everything private and local; do not reference or call any external services.
- Use only the provided input files and your generated content.
- Keep all writing in English.
